commands.review_summary:
Adds a backend-delegated user-only transcript warning to `/export-session` replies and exported HTML, with CSS and command-level regression tests.

PR surface: Source +33, Tests +58. Total +91 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: Source-reproducible, not live-reproduced in this review: current main exports only OpenClaw transcript entries, and the linked issue plus CLI user-turn persistence path support the user-only transcript shape. A high-confidence merge proof still needs a real CLI/ACP-backed `/export-session` run.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor follow-up is needed for real behavior proof; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue from this review.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): warn when /export-session only contains user messages (backend-delegated)
target.body:
## Summary When a session is driven by a CLI/ACP backend (e.g. Claude Code CLI), OpenClaw's own transcript only records the inbound user messages. `/export-session` would still produce a \"successful\" HTML export with empty summary stats and no assistant/tool/usage data, which is misleading. This change detects the backend-delegated case (a transcript whose message entries are exclusively `role: user`) and surfaces a clear warning in: - the command reply text, and - the exported HTML header (via a new `warning` field in the session data payload). Closes #90844 (minimum-fix option 2). ## Changes - `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-export-session.ts` - Adds `isBackendDelegatedSession()` guard. - Embeds `warning` into `SessionData` and the command reply text when the guard fires. - `src/auto-reply/reply/export-html/template.js` - Renders the warning as a visible banner above the session header. - `src/auto-reply/reply/export-html/template.css` - Styles the warning banner with the existing warning color. - `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-export-session.test.ts` - Adds regression tests for the warning (user-only transcript) and the non-warning case (mixed transcript). ## Verification - `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/commands-export-session.test.ts` — 9/9 pass. - `pnpm build` — completed successfully (export HTML templates copied, CLI/bootstrap checks pass). - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` on changed TS/JS/CSS files — clean. - `pnpm dlx oxlint@latest -c .oxlintrc.json` on changed TS files — clean. ## Real behavior proof **Behavior addressed:** `/export-session` produces a misleading successful export for backend-delegated sessions whose transcript contains only `role: user` messages, with no warning that assistant/tool/usage data are missing. **Real environment tested:** Local Linux checkout of this branch, Node v22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2. **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ```bash cd /home/0668000780/开源社区提交2/openclaw git checkout fix/export-session-backend-delegated-warning node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/repro-90867.proof.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose ``` The proof test builds a user-only transcript (`hello`, `world`), invokes `buildExportSessionReply` with `/export-session /tmp/repro-90867-output.html`, and inspects both the command reply text and the generated HTML. **Evidence after fix:** ``` stdout | src/auto-reply/reply/repro-90867.proof.test.ts > real behavior proof #90867 > exports backend-delegated session with warning === Reply text === ✅ Session exported! 📄 File: /tmp/repro-90867-output.html 📊 Entries: 2 ⚠️ This session was handled by a backend runtime (e.g. CLI/ACP). Assistant replies, tool calls, and usage data are stored in the backend transcript and are not included in this export. 🧠 System prompt: 13 chars 🔧 Tools: 0 === HTML warning banner snippet ===
${escapeHtml(warning)}
=== sessionData.warning === This session was handled by a backend runtime (e.g. CLI/ACP). Assistant replies, tool calls, and usage data are stored in the backend transcript and are not included in this export. ``` **Observed result after fix:** The command reply now includes the backend-delegated warning, the exported HTML embeds `warning` in the base64 session data payload, and `template.js` renders it through the `
` banner. **What was not tested:** Loading the generated HTML in a browser to visually confirm the banner rendering; the rendering path is covered by the `export-warning` template markup and the regression tests.
target.author: xydigit-sj
